text:
There once was a cat who had a deep fear of spiders. Every single time she saw a spider, she would start bawling.

One day, she decided she had finally had enough. She gathered her courage and went to the spider's web anyway. When she approached, the spider simply stared at her in silent disbelief. The cat licked her lips and inched closer. The spider didn't flinch—an incredible accomplishment for its species.

Taking advantage of her boldness, the cat gently sat down on the web. Somehow, bravely overcoming her fear, she managed a sigh.

"Isn't that... um... alright?" she ventured.

The spider felt humbled. It nodded.

After that, the cat spent every day near the web. At first, she stared at the spider, her tears flowing, but gradually, curiosity won over fear. Eventually, she decided to reach out for a piece of fly. She pounced!

The spider was amused. For the first time ever,
